mappings.rationale: Directly covers legal systems, the practice of law, and foundational concepts of jurisprudence.
Directly covers Western philosophy of law, jurisprudence theories, and the relationship between law and morality (subsection A.1).
Covers the development of Anglo-American common law as a modern legal system (subsection B.4.a).
Directly covers the study of distinctions and parallels among diverse legal systems (subsection C).
Covers the foundational principle governing legal systems and its relationship to constitutional government.
Covers Islamic law as a major non-Western legal philosophy and system (subsection A.2).
Covers Islamic philosophical traditions relevant to non-Western philosophies of law (subsection A.2).
Covers Greek law as an ancient legal system (subsection B.2.d).
Covers the development of Roman law as a foundational ancient legal system (subsection B.2.f).
Covers Roman law during the imperial period, foundational to Continental civil law.
Covers Egyptian law as an ancient legal system (subsection B.2.a).
Covers cuneiform law in ancient Near Eastern civilizations (subsection B.2.b).
Covers medieval European law including constitutional and private law institutions (subsection B.3).
Covers a foundational document of medieval constitutional law and the common law tradition (subsection B.3.b).
Covers the relationship between law and morality in the medical domain, relevant to subsection A.1.c.
Covers the philosophical relationship between law and morality and natural law principles (subsection A.1.c).
Covers natural law theory as a major tradition in the philosophy of law (subsection A.1.c).
Covers Chinese legal philosophy as a non-Western legal tradition (subsection A.2).
Covers classical Greek and Roman legal traditions foundational to Western law.
Covers Stoic natural law philosophy foundational to Western legal theory.
Covers Hobbesian legal theory including the social contract and the basis of legal authority.
Covers Lockean theories of natural rights and their influence on legal philosophy.
